2432	apply_error_callback_arg.rel = rel;
2433
2434	/* Initialize the executor state. */
2435	edata = create_edata_for_relation(rel);
2436	estate = edata->estate;
2437	remoteslot = ExecInitExtraTupleSlot(estate,
2438										RelationGetDescr(rel->localrel),
2439										&TTSOpsVirtual);
2440
2441	/* Process and store remote tuple in the slot */
2442	oldctx = MemoryContextSwitchTo(GetPerTupleMemoryContext(estate));
2443	slot_store_data(remoteslot, rel, &newtup);
2444	slot_fill_defaults(rel, estate, remoteslot);
2445	MemoryContextSwitchTo(oldctx);
2446
2447	/* For a partitioned table, insert the tuple into a partition. */
2448	if (rel->localrel->rd_rel->relkind == RELKIND_PARTITIONED_TABLE)
2449		apply_handle_tuple_routing(edata,
2450								   remoteslot, NULL, CMD_INSERT);
2451	else
2452	{
2453		ResultRelInfo *relinfo = edata->targetRelInfo;
2454
2455		ExecOpenIndices(relinfo, false);
2456		apply_handle_insert_internal(edata, relinfo, remoteslot);
2457		ExecCloseIndices(relinfo);
2458	}
2459
2460	finish_edata(edata);
2461
2462	/* Reset relation for error callback */
2463	apply_error_callback_arg.rel = NULL;
2464
2465	if (!run_as_owner)
2466		RestoreUserContext(&ucxt);
2467
2468	logicalrep_rel_close(rel, NoLock);
2469
2470	end_replication_step();
2471}
2472
2473/*
2474 * Workhorse for apply_handle_insert()
2475 * relinfo is for the relation we're actually inserting into
2476 * (could be a child partition of edata->targetRelInfo)
2477 */
2478static void
2479apply_handle_insert_internal(ApplyExecutionData *edata,
2480							 ResultRelInfo *relinfo,
2481							 TupleTableSlot *remoteslot)
2482{
2483	EState	   *estate = edata->estate;
2484
2485	/* Caller should have opened indexes already. */
2486	Assert(relinfo->ri_IndexRelationDescs != NULL ||
2487		   !relinfo->ri_RelationDesc->rd_rel->relhasindex ||
2488		   RelationGetIndexList(relinfo->ri_RelationDesc) == NIL);
2489
2490	/* Caller will not have done this bit. */
2491	Assert(relinfo->ri_onConflictArbiterIndexes == NIL);
2492	InitConflictIndexes(relinfo);
2493
2494	/* Do the insert. */
2495	TargetPrivilegesCheck(relinfo->ri_RelationDesc, ACL_INSERT);
2496	ExecSimpleRelationInsert(relinfo, estate, remoteslot);
2497}
2498
2499/*
2500 * Check if the logical replication relation is updatable and throw
2501 * appropriate error if it isn't.
2502 */
2503static void
2504check_relation_updatable(LogicalRepRelMapEntry *rel)
2505{
2506	/*
2507	 * For partitioned tables, we only need to care if the target partition is
2508	 * updatable (aka has PK or RI defined for it).
2509	 */
2510	if (rel->localrel->rd_rel->relkind == RELKIND_PARTITIONED_TABLE)
2511		return;
2512
2513	/* Updatable, no error. */
2514	if (rel->updatable)
2515		return;
2516
2517	/*
2518	 * We are in error mode so it's fine this is somewhat slow. It's better to
2519	 * give user correct error.
2520	 */
2521	if (OidIsValid(GetRelationIdentityOrPK(rel->localrel)))
2522	{
2523		ereport(ERROR,
2524				(errcode(ERRCODE_OBJECT_NOT_IN_PREREQUISITE_STATE),
2525				 errmsg("publisher did not send replica identity column "
2526						"expected by the logical replication target relation \"%s.%s\"",
2527						rel->remoterel.nspname, rel->remoterel.relname)));
2528	}
2529
2530	ereport(ERROR,
2531			(errcode(ERRCODE_OBJECT_NOT_IN_PREREQUISITE_STATE),
2532			 errmsg("logical replication target relation \"%s.%s\" has "
2533					"neither REPLICA IDENTITY index nor PRIMARY "
2534					"KEY and published relation does not have "
2535					"REPLICA IDENTITY FULL",
2536					rel->remoterel.nspname, rel->remoterel.relname)));
2537}
2538
2539/*
2540 * Handle UPDATE message.
2541 *
2542 * TODO: FDW support
2543 */
2544static void
2545apply_handle_update(StringInfo s)
2546{
2547	LogicalRepRelMapEntry *rel;
2548	LogicalRepRelId relid;
2549	UserContext ucxt;
2550	ApplyExecutionData *edata;
2551	EState	   *estate;
2552	LogicalRepTupleData oldtup;
2553	LogicalRepTupleData newtup;
2554	bool		has_oldtup;
2555	TupleTableSlot *remoteslot;
2556	RTEPermissionInfo *target_perminfo;
2557	MemoryContext oldctx;
2558	bool		run_as_owner;
2559
2560	/*
2561	 * Quick return if we are skipping data modification changes or handling
2562	 * streamed transactions.
2563	 */
2564	if (is_skipping_changes() ||
2565		handle_streamed_transaction(LOGICAL_REP_MSG_UPDATE, s))
2566		return;
2567
2568	begin_replication_step();
2569
2570	relid = logicalrep_read_update(s, &has_oldtup, &oldtup,
2571								   &newtup);
2572	rel = logicalrep_rel_open(relid, RowExclusiveLock);
2573	if (!should_apply_changes_for_rel(rel))
2574	{
2575		/*
2576		 * The relation can't become interesting in the middle of the
2577		 * transaction so it's safe to unlock it.
2578		 */
2579		logicalrep_rel_close(rel, RowExclusiveLock);
2580		end_replication_step();
2581		return;
2582	}
2583
2584	/* Set relation for error callback */
2585	apply_error_callback_arg.rel = rel;
2586
2587	/* Check if we can do the update. */
2588	check_relation_updatable(rel);
2589
2590	/*
2591	 * Make sure that any user-supplied code runs as the table owner, unless
2592	 * the user has opted out of that behavior.
2593	 */
2594	run_as_owner = MySubscription->runasowner;
2595	if (!run_as_owner)
2596		SwitchToUntrustedUser(rel->localrel->rd_rel->relowner, &ucxt);
2597
2598	/* Initialize the executor state. */
2599	edata = create_edata_for_relation(rel);
2600	estate = edata->estate;
2601	remoteslot = ExecInitExtraTupleSlot(estate,
2602										RelationGetDescr(rel->localrel),
2603										&TTSOpsVirtual);
2604
2605	/*
2606	 * Populate updatedCols so that per-column triggers can fire, and so
2607	 * executor can correctly pass down indexUnchanged hint.  This could
2608	 * include more columns than were actually changed on the publisher
2609	 * because the logical replication protocol doesn't contain that
2610	 * information.  But it would for example exclude columns that only exist
2611	 * on the subscriber, since we are not touching those.
2612	 */
2613	target_perminfo = list_nth(estate->es_rteperminfos, 0);
2614	for (int i = 0; i < remoteslot->tts_tupleDescriptor->natts; i++)
2615	{
2616		Form_pg_attribute att = TupleDescAttr(remoteslot->tts_tupleDescriptor, i);
2617		int			remoteattnum = rel->attrmap->attnums[i];
2618
2619		if (!att->attisdropped && remoteattnum >= 0)
2620		{
2621			Assert(remoteattnum < newtup.ncols);
2622			if (newtup.colstatus[remoteattnum] != LOGICALREP_COLUMN_UNCHANGED)
2623				target_perminfo->updatedCols =
2624					bms_add_member(target_perminfo->updatedCols,
2625								   i + 1 - FirstLowInvalidHeapAttributeNumber);
2626		}
2627	}
2628
2629	/* Build the search tuple. */
2630	oldctx = MemoryContextSwitchTo(GetPerTupleMemoryContext(estate));
2631	slot_store_data(remoteslot, rel,
2632					has_oldtup ? &oldtup : &newtup);
2633	MemoryContextSwitchTo(oldctx);
2634
2635	/* For a partitioned table, apply update to correct partition. */
2636	if (rel->localrel->rd_rel->relkind == RELKIND_PARTITIONED_TABLE)
2637		apply_handle_tuple_routing(edata,
2638								   remoteslot, &newtup, CMD_UPDATE);
2639	else
2640		apply_handle_update_internal(edata, edata->targetRelInfo,
2641									 remoteslot, &newtup, rel->localindexoid);
2642
2643	finish_edata(edata);
2644
2645	/* Reset relation for error callback */
2646	apply_error_callback_arg.rel = NULL;
2647
2648	if (!run_as_owner)
2649		RestoreUserContext(&ucxt);
2650
2651	logicalrep_rel_close(rel, NoLock);
2652
2653	end_replication_step();
2654}
2655
2656/*
2657 * Workhorse for apply_handle_update()
2658 * relinfo is for the relation we're actually updating in
2659 * (could be a child partition of edata->targetRelInfo)
2660 */
2661static void
2662apply_handle_update_internal(ApplyExecutionData *edata,
2663							 ResultRelInfo *relinfo,
2664							 TupleTableSlot *remoteslot,
2665							 LogicalRepTupleData *newtup,
2666							 Oid localindexoid)
2667{
2668	EState	   *estate = edata->estate;
2669	LogicalRepRelMapEntry *relmapentry = edata->targetRel;
2670	Relation	localrel = relinfo->ri_RelationDesc;
2671	EPQState	epqstate;
2672	TupleTableSlot *localslot = NULL;
2673	ConflictTupleInfo conflicttuple = {0};
2674	bool		found;
2675	MemoryContext oldctx;
2676
2677	EvalPlanQualInit(&epqstate, estate, NULL, NIL, -1, NIL);
2678	ExecOpenIndices(relinfo, false);
2679
2680	found = FindReplTupleInLocalRel(edata, localrel,
2681									&relmapentry->remoterel,
2682									localindexoid,
2683									remoteslot, &localslot);
2684
2685	/*
2686	 * Tuple found.
2687	 *
2688	 * Note this will fail if there are other conflicting unique indexes.
2689	 */
2690	if (found)
2691	{
2692		/*
2693		 * Report the conflict if the tuple was modified by a different
2694		 * origin.
2695		 */
2696		if (GetTupleTransactionInfo(localslot, &conflicttuple.xmin,
2697									&conflicttuple.origin, &conflicttuple.ts) &&
2698			conflicttuple.origin != replorigin_session_origin)
2699		{
2700			TupleTableSlot *newslot;
2701
2702			/* Store the new tuple for conflict reporting */
2703			newslot = table_slot_create(localrel, &estate->es_tupleTable);
2704			slot_store_data(newslot, relmapentry, newtup);
2705
2706			conflicttuple.slot = localslot;
2707
2708			ReportApplyConflict(estate, relinfo, LOG, CT_UPDATE_ORIGIN_DIFFERS,
2709								remoteslot, newslot,
2710								list_make1(&conflicttuple));
2711		}
2712
2713		/* Process and store remote tuple in the slot */
2714		oldctx = MemoryContextSwitchTo(GetPerTupleMemoryContext(estate));
2715		slot_modify_data(remoteslot, localslot, relmapentry, newtup);
2716		MemoryContextSwitchTo(oldctx);
2717
2718		EvalPlanQualSetSlot(&epqstate, remoteslot);
2719
2720		InitConflictIndexes(relinfo);
2721
2722		/* Do the actual update. */
2723		TargetPrivilegesCheck(relinfo->ri_RelationDesc, ACL_UPDATE);
2724		ExecSimpleRelationUpdate(relinfo, estate, &epqstate, localslot,
2725								 remoteslot);
2726	}
2727	else
2728	{
2729		TupleTableSlot *newslot = localslot;
2730
2731		/* Store the new tuple for conflict reporting */
2732		slot_store_data(newslot, relmapentry, newtup);
2733
2734		/*
2735		 * The tuple to be updated could not be found.  Do nothing except for
2736		 * emitting a log message.
2737		 */
2738		ReportApplyConflict(estate, relinfo, LOG, CT_UPDATE_MISSING,
2739							remoteslot, newslot, list_make1(&conflicttuple));
2740	}
2741
2742	/* Cleanup. */
2743	ExecCloseIndices(relinfo);
2744	EvalPlanQualEnd(&epqstate);
2745}
2746
2747/*
2748 * Handle DELETE message.
2749 *
2750 * TODO: FDW support
2751 */
2752static void
2753apply_handle_delete(StringInfo s)
2754{
2755	LogicalRepRelMapEntry *rel;
2756	LogicalRepTupleData oldtup;
2757	LogicalRepRelId relid;
2758	UserContext ucxt;
2759	ApplyExecutionData *edata;
2760	EState	   *estate;
2761	TupleTableSlot *remoteslot;
2762	MemoryContext oldctx;
2763	bool		run_as_owner;
2764
2765	/*
2766	 * Quick return if we are skipping data modification changes or handling
2767	 * streamed transactions.
2768	 */
2769	if (is_skipping_changes() ||
2770		handle_streamed_transaction(LOGICAL_REP_MSG_DELETE, s))
2771		return;
2772
2773	begin_replication_step();
2774
2775	relid = logicalrep_read_delete(s, &oldtup);
2776	rel = logicalrep_rel_open(relid, RowExclusiveLock);
2777	if (!should_apply_changes_for_rel(rel))
2778	{
2779		/*
2780		 * The relation can't become interesting in the middle of the
2781		 * transaction so it's safe to unlock it.
2782		 */
2783		logicalrep_rel_close(rel, RowExclusiveLock);
2784		end_replication_step();
2785		return;
2786	}
2787
2788	/* Set relation for error callback */
2789	apply_error_callback_arg.rel = rel;
2790
2791	/* Check if we can do the delete. */
2792	check_relation_updatable(rel);
2793
2794	/*
2795	 * Make sure that any user-supplied code runs as the table owner, unless
2796	 * the user has opted out of that behavior.
2797	 */
2798	run_as_owner = MySubscription->runasowner;
2799	if (!run_as_owner)
2800		SwitchToUntrustedUser(rel->localrel->rd_rel->relowner, &ucxt);
2801
2802	/* Initialize the executor state. */
2803	edata = create_edata_for_relation(rel);
2804	estate = edata->estate;
2805	remoteslot = ExecInitExtraTupleSlot(estate,
2806										RelationGetDescr(rel->localrel),
2807										&TTSOpsVirtual);
2808
2809	/* Build the search tuple. */
2810	oldctx = MemoryContextSwitchTo(GetPerTupleMemoryContext(estate));
2811	slot_store_data(remoteslot, rel, &oldtup);
2812	MemoryContextSwitchTo(oldctx);
2813
2814	/* For a partitioned table, apply delete to correct partition. */
2815	if (rel->localrel->rd_rel->relkind == RELKIND_PARTITIONED_TABLE)
2816		apply_handle_tuple_routing(edata,
2817								   remoteslot, NULL, CMD_DELETE);
2818	else
2819	{
2820		ResultRelInfo *relinfo = edata->targetRelInfo;
2821
2822		ExecOpenIndices(relinfo, false);
2823		apply_handle_delete_internal(edata, relinfo,
2824									 remoteslot, rel->localindexoid);
2825		ExecCloseIndices(relinfo);
2826	}
2827
2828	finish_edata(edata);
2829
2830	/* Reset relation for error callback */
2831	apply_error_callback_arg.rel = NULL;
2832
2833	if (!run_as_owner)
2834		RestoreUserContext(&ucxt);
2835
2836	logicalrep_rel_close(rel, NoLock);
2837
2838	end_replication_step();
2839}
2840
2841/*
2842 * Workhorse for apply_handle_delete()
2843 * relinfo is for the relation we're actually deleting from
2844 * (could be a child partition of edata->targetRelInfo)
2845 */
2846static void
2847apply_handle_delete_internal(ApplyExecutionData *edata,
2848							 ResultRelInfo *relinfo,
2849							 TupleTableSlot *remoteslot,
2850							 Oid localindexoid)
2851{
2852	EState	   *estate = edata->estate;
2853	Relation	localrel = relinfo->ri_RelationDesc;
2854	LogicalRepRelation *remoterel = &edata->targetRel->remoterel;
2855	EPQState	epqstate;
2856	TupleTableSlot *localslot;
2857	ConflictTupleInfo conflicttuple = {0};
2858	bool		found;
2859
2860	EvalPlanQualInit(&epqstate, estate, NULL, NIL, -1, NIL);
2861
2862	/* Caller should have opened indexes already. */
2863	Assert(relinfo->ri_IndexRelationDescs != NULL ||
2864		   !localrel->rd_rel->relhasindex ||
2865		   RelationGetIndexList(localrel) == NIL);
2866
2867	found = FindReplTupleInLocalRel(edata, localrel, remoterel, localindexoid,
2868									remoteslot, &localslot);
2869
2870	/* If found delete it. */
2871	if (found)
2872	{
2873		/*
2874		 * Report the conflict if the tuple was modified by a different
2875		 * origin.
2876		 */
2877		if (GetTupleTransactionInfo(localslot, &conflicttuple.xmin,
2878									&conflicttuple.origin, &conflicttuple.ts) &&
2879			conflicttuple.origin != replorigin_session_origin)
2880		{
2881			conflicttuple.slot = localslot;
2882			ReportApplyConflict(estate, relinfo, LOG, CT_DELETE_ORIGIN_DIFFERS,
2883								remoteslot, NULL,
2884								list_make1(&conflicttuple));
2885		}
2886
2887		EvalPlanQualSetSlot(&epqstate, localslot);
2888
2889		/* Do the actual delete. */
2890		TargetPrivilegesCheck(relinfo->ri_RelationDesc, ACL_DELETE);
2891		ExecSimpleRelationDelete(relinfo, estate, &epqstate, localslot);
2892	}
2893	else
2894	{
2895		/*
2896		 * The tuple to be deleted could not be found.  Do nothing except for
2897		 * emitting a log message.
2898		 */
2899		ReportApplyConflict(estate, relinfo, LOG, CT_DELETE_MISSING,
2900							remoteslot, NULL, list_make1(&conflicttuple));
2901	}
2902
2903	/* Cleanup. */
2904	EvalPlanQualEnd(&epqstate);
2905}
2906
2907/*
2908 * Try to find a tuple received from the publication side (in 'remoteslot') in
2909 * the corresponding local relation using either replica identity index,
2910 * primary key, index or if needed, sequential scan.
2911 *
2912 * Local tuple, if found, is returned in '*localslot'.
2913 */
2914static bool
2915FindReplTupleInLocalRel(ApplyExecutionData *edata, Relation localrel,
2916						LogicalRepRelation *remoterel,
2917						Oid localidxoid,
2918						TupleTableSlot *remoteslot,
2919						TupleTableSlot **localslot)
2920{
2921	EState	   *estate = edata->estate;
2922	bool		found;
2923
2924	/*
2925	 * Regardless of the top-level operation, we're performing a read here, so
2926	 * check for SELECT privileges.
2927	 */
2928	TargetPrivilegesCheck(localrel, ACL_SELECT);
2929
2930	*localslot = table_slot_create(localrel, &estate->es_tupleTable);
2931
2932	Assert(OidIsValid(localidxoid) ||
2933		   (remoterel->replident == REPLICA_IDENTITY_FULL));
2934
2935	if (OidIsValid(localidxoid))
2936	{
2937#ifdef USE_ASSERT_CHECKING
2938		Relation	idxrel = index_open(localidxoid, AccessShareLock);
2939
2940		/* Index must be PK, RI, or usable for REPLICA IDENTITY FULL tables */
2941		Assert(GetRelationIdentityOrPK(localrel) == localidxoid ||
2942			   (remoterel->replident == REPLICA_IDENTITY_FULL &&
2943				IsIndexUsableForReplicaIdentityFull(idxrel,
2944													edata->targetRel->attrmap)));
2945		index_close(idxrel, AccessShareLock);
2946#endif
2947
2948		found = RelationFindReplTupleByIndex(localrel, localidxoid,
2949											 LockTupleExclusive,
2950											 remoteslot, *localslot);
2951	}
2952	else
2953		found = RelationFindReplTupleSeq(localrel, LockTupleExclusive,
2954										 remoteslot, *localslot);
2955
2956	return found;
2957}
2958
2959/*
2960 * This handles insert, update, delete on a partitioned table.
2961 */
2962static void
2963apply_handle_tuple_routing(ApplyExecutionData *edata,
2964						   TupleTableSlot *remoteslot,
2965						   LogicalRepTupleData *newtup,
2966						   CmdType operation)
2967{
2968	EState	   *estate = edata->estate;
2969	LogicalRepRelMapEntry *relmapentry = edata->targetRel;
2970	ResultRelInfo *relinfo = edata->targetRelInfo;
2971	Relation	parentrel = relinfo->ri_RelationDesc;
2972	ModifyTableState *mtstate;
2973	PartitionTupleRouting *proute;
2974	ResultRelInfo *partrelinfo;
2975	Relation	partrel;
2976	TupleTableSlot *remoteslot_part;
2977	TupleConversionMap *map;
2978	MemoryContext oldctx;
2979	LogicalRepRelMapEntry *part_entry = NULL;
2980	AttrMap    *attrmap = NULL;
2981
2982	/* ModifyTableState is needed for ExecFindPartition(). */
2983	edata->mtstate = mtstate = makeNode(ModifyTableState);
2984	mtstate->ps.plan = NULL;
2985	mtstate->ps.state = estate;
2986	mtstate->operation = operation;
2987	mtstate->resultRelInfo = relinfo;
2988
2989	/* ... as is PartitionTupleRouting. */
2990	edata->proute = proute = ExecSetupPartitionTupleRouting(estate, parentrel);
2991
2992	/*
2993	 * Find the partition to which the "search tuple" belongs.
2994	 */
2995	Assert(remoteslot != NULL);
2996	oldctx = MemoryContextSwitchTo(GetPerTupleMemoryContext(estate));
2997	partrelinfo = ExecFindPartition(mtstate, relinfo, proute,
2998									remoteslot, estate);
2999	Assert(partrelinfo != NULL);
3000	partrel = partrelinfo->ri_RelationDesc;
3001
3002	/*
3003	 * Check for supported relkind.  We need this since partitions might be of
3004	 * unsupported relkinds; and the set of partitions can change, so checking
3005	 * at CREATE/ALTER SUBSCRIPTION would be insufficient.
3006	 */
3007	CheckSubscriptionRelkind(partrel->rd_rel->relkind,
3008							 get_namespace_name(RelationGetNamespace(partrel)),
3009							 RelationGetRelationName(partrel));
3010
3011	/*
3012	 * To perform any of the operations below, the tuple must match the
3013	 * partition's rowtype. Convert if needed or just copy, using a dedicated
3014	 * slot to store the tuple in any case.
3015	 */
3016	remoteslot_part = partrelinfo->ri_PartitionTupleSlot;
3017	if (remoteslot_part == NULL)
3018		remoteslot_part = table_slot_create(partrel, &estate->es_tupleTable);
3019	map = ExecGetRootToChildMap(partrelinfo, estate);
3020	if (map != NULL)
3021	{
3022		attrmap = map->attrMap;
3023		remoteslot_part = execute_attr_map_slot(attrmap, remoteslot,
3024												remoteslot_part);
3025	}
3026	else
3027	{
3028		remoteslot_part = ExecCopySlot(remoteslot_part, remoteslot);
3029		slot_getallattrs(remoteslot_part);
3030	}
3031	MemoryContextSwitchTo(oldctx);
3032
3033	/* Check if we can do the update or delete on the leaf partition. */
3034	if (operation == CMD_UPDATE || operation == CMD_DELETE)
3035	{
3036		part_entry = logicalrep_partition_open(relmapentry, partrel,
3037											   attrmap);
3038		check_relation_updatable(part_entry);
3039	}
3040
3041	switch (operation)
3042	{
3043		case CMD_INSERT:
3044			apply_handle_insert_internal(edata, partrelinfo,
3045										 remoteslot_part);
3046			break;
3047
3048		case CMD_DELETE:
3049			apply_handle_delete_internal(edata, partrelinfo,
3050										 remoteslot_part,
3051										 part_entry->localindexoid);
3052			break;
3053
3054		case CMD_UPDATE:
3055
3056			/*
3057			 * For UPDATE, depending on whether or not the updated tuple
3058			 * satisfies the partition's constraint, perform a simple UPDATE
3059			 * of the partition or move the updated tuple into a different
3060			 * suitable partition.
3061			 */
3062			{
3063				TupleTableSlot *localslot;
3064				ResultRelInfo *partrelinfo_new;
3065				Relation	partrel_new;
3066				bool		found;
3067				EPQState	epqstate;
3068				ConflictTupleInfo conflicttuple = {0};
3069
3070				/* Get the matching local tuple from the partition. */
3071				found = FindReplTupleInLocalRel(edata, partrel,
3072												&part_entry->remoterel,
3073												part_entry->localindexoid,
3074												remoteslot_part, &localslot);
3075				if (!found)
3076				{
3077					TupleTableSlot *newslot = localslot;
3078
3079					/* Store the new tuple for conflict reporting */
3080					slot_store_data(newslot, part_entry, newtup);
3081
3082					/*
3083					 * The tuple to be updated could not be found.  Do nothing
3084					 * except for emitting a log message.
3085					 */
3086					ReportApplyConflict(estate, partrelinfo, LOG,
3087										CT_UPDATE_MISSING, remoteslot_part,
3088										newslot, list_make1(&conflicttuple));
3089
3090					return;
3091				}
3092
3093				/*
3094				 * Report the conflict if the tuple was modified by a
3095				 * different origin.
3096				 */
3097				if (GetTupleTransactionInfo(localslot, &conflicttuple.xmin,
3098											&conflicttuple.origin,
3099											&conflicttuple.ts) &&
3100					conflicttuple.origin != replorigin_session_origin)
3101				{
3102					TupleTableSlot *newslot;
3103
3104					/* Store the new tuple for conflict reporting */
3105					newslot = table_slot_create(partrel, &estate->es_tupleTable);
3106					slot_store_data(newslot, part_entry, newtup);
3107
3108					conflicttuple.slot = localslot;
3109
3110					ReportApplyConflict(estate, partrelinfo, LOG, CT_UPDATE_ORIGIN_DIFFERS,
3111										remoteslot_part, newslot,
3112										list_make1(&conflicttuple));
3113				}
3114
3115				/*
3116				 * Apply the update to the local tuple, putting the result in
3117				 * remoteslot_part.
3118				 */
3119				oldctx = MemoryContextSwitchTo(GetPerTupleMemoryContext(estate));
3120				slot_modify_data(remoteslot_part, localslot, part_entry,
3121								 newtup);
3122				MemoryContextSwitchTo(oldctx);
3123
3124				EvalPlanQualInit(&epqstate, estate, NULL, NIL, -1, NIL);
3125
3126				/*
3127				 * Does the updated tuple still satisfy the current
3128				 * partition's constraint?
3129				 */
3130				if (!partrel->rd_rel->relispartition ||
3131					ExecPartitionCheck(partrelinfo, remoteslot_part, estate,
3132									   false))
3133				{
3134					/*
3135					 * Yes, so simply UPDATE the partition.  We don't call
3136					 * apply_handle_update_internal() here, which would
3137					 * normally do the following work, to avoid repeating some
3138					 * work already done above to find the local tuple in the
3139					 * partition.
3140					 */
3141					InitConflictIndexes(partrelinfo);
3142
3143					EvalPlanQualSetSlot(&epqstate, remoteslot_part);
3144					TargetPrivilegesCheck(partrelinfo->ri_RelationDesc,
3145										  ACL_UPDATE);
3146					ExecSimpleRelationUpdate(partrelinfo, estate, &epqstate,
3147											 localslot, remoteslot_part);
3148				}
3149				else
3150				{
3151					/* Move the tuple into the new partition. */
3152
3153					/*
3154					 * New partition will be found using tuple routing, which
3155					 * can only occur via the parent table.  We might need to
3156					 * convert the tuple to the parent's rowtype.  Note that
3157					 * this is the tuple found in the partition, not the
3158					 * original search tuple received by this function.
3159					 */
3160					if (map)
3161					{
3162						TupleConversionMap *PartitionToRootMap =
3163							convert_tuples_by_name(RelationGetDescr(partrel),
3164												   RelationGetDescr(parentrel));
3165
3166						remoteslot =
3167							execute_attr_map_slot(PartitionToRootMap->attrMap,
3168												  remoteslot_part, remoteslot);
3169					}
3170					else
3171					{
3172						remoteslot = ExecCopySlot(remoteslot, remoteslot_part);
3173						slot_getallattrs(remoteslot);
3174					}
3175
3176					/* Find the new partition. */
3177					oldctx = MemoryContextSwitchTo(GetPerTupleMemoryContext(estate));
3178					partrelinfo_new = ExecFindPartition(mtstate, relinfo,
3179														proute, remoteslot,
3180														estate);
3181					MemoryContextSwitchTo(oldctx);
3182					Assert(partrelinfo_new != partrelinfo);
3183					partrel_new = partrelinfo_new->ri_RelationDesc;
3184
3185					/* Check that new partition also has supported relkind. */
3186					CheckSubscriptionRelkind(partrel_new->rd_rel->relkind,
3187											 get_namespace_name(RelationGetNamespace(partrel_new)),
3188											 RelationGetRelationName(partrel_new));
3189
3190					/* DELETE old tuple found in the old partition. */
3191					EvalPlanQualSetSlot(&epqstate, localslot);
3192					TargetPrivilegesCheck(partrelinfo->ri_RelationDesc, ACL_DELETE);
3193					ExecSimpleRelationDelete(partrelinfo, estate, &epqstate, localslot);
3194
3195					/* INSERT new tuple into the new partition. */
3196
3197					/*
3198					 * Convert the replacement tuple to match the destination
3199					 * partition rowtype.
3200					 */
3201					oldctx = MemoryContextSwitchTo(GetPerTupleMemoryContext(estate));
3202					remoteslot_part = partrelinfo_new->ri_PartitionTupleSlot;
3203					if (remoteslot_part == NULL)
3204						remoteslot_part = table_slot_create(partrel_new,
3205															&estate->es_tupleTable);
3206					map = ExecGetRootToChildMap(partrelinfo_new, estate);
3207					if (map != NULL)
3208					{
3209						remoteslot_part = execute_attr_map_slot(map->attrMap,
3210																remoteslot,
3211																remoteslot_part);
3212					}
3213					else
3214					{
3215						remoteslot_part = ExecCopySlot(remoteslot_part,
3216													   remoteslot);
3217						slot_getallattrs(remoteslot);
3218					}
3219					MemoryContextSwitchTo(oldctx);
3220					apply_handle_insert_internal(edata, partrelinfo_new,
3221												 remoteslot_part);
3222				}
3223
3224				EvalPlanQualEnd(&epqstate);
3225			}
3226			break;
3227
3228		default:
3229			elog(ERROR, "unrecognized CmdType: %d", (int) operation);
3230			break;
3231	}
3232}
3233
3234/*
3235 * Handle TRUNCATE message.
3236 *
3237 * TODO: FDW support
3238 */
3239static void
3240apply_handle_truncate(StringInfo s)
3241{
3242	bool		cascade = false;
3243	bool		restart_seqs = false;
3244	List	   *remote_relids = NIL;
3245	List	   *remote_rels = NIL;
3246	List	   *rels = NIL;
3247	List	   *part_rels = NIL;
3248	List	   *relids = NIL;
3249	List	   *relids_logged = NIL;
3250	ListCell   *lc;
3251	LOCKMODE	lockmode = AccessExclusiveLock;
3252
3253	/*
3254	 * Quick return if we are skipping data modification changes or handling
3255	 * streamed transactions.
3256	 */
3257	if (is_skipping_changes() ||
3258		handle_streamed_transaction(LOGICAL_REP_MSG_TRUNCATE, s))
3259		return;
3260
3261	begin_replication_step();
3262
3263	remote_relids = logicalrep_read_truncate(s, &cascade, &restart_seqs);
3264
3265	foreach(lc, remote_relids)
3266	{
3267		LogicalRepRelId relid = lfirst_oid(lc);
3268		LogicalRepRelMapEntry *rel;
3269
3270		rel = logicalrep_rel_open(relid, lockmode);
3271		if (!should_apply_changes_for_rel(rel))
3272		{
3273			/*
3274			 * The relation can't become interesting in the middle of the
3275			 * transaction so it's safe to unlock it.
3276			 */
3277			logicalrep_rel_close(rel, lockmode);
3278			continue;
3279		}
3280
3281		remote_rels = lappend(remote_rels, rel);
3282		TargetPrivilegesCheck(rel->localrel, ACL_TRUNCATE);
3283		rels = lappend(rels, rel->localrel);
3284		relids = lappend_oid(relids, rel->localreloid);
3285		if (RelationIsLogicallyLogged(rel->localrel))
3286			relids_logged = lappend_oid(relids_logged, rel->localreloid);
3287
3288		/*
3289		 * Truncate partitions if we got a message to truncate a partitioned
3290		 * table.
3291		 */
3292		if (rel->localrel->rd_rel->relkind == RELKIND_PARTITIONED_TABLE)
3293		{
3294			ListCell   *child;
3295			List	   *children = find_all_inheritors(rel->localreloid,
3296													   lockmode,
3297													   NULL);
3298
3299			foreach(child, children)
3300			{
3301				Oid			childrelid = lfirst_oid(child);
3302				Relation	childrel;
3303
3304				if (list_member_oid(relids, childrelid))
3305					continue;
3306
3307				/* find_all_inheritors already got lock */
3308				childrel = table_open(childrelid, NoLock);
3309
3310				/*
3311				 * Ignore temp tables of other backends.  See similar code in
3312				 * ExecuteTruncate().
3313				 */
3314				if (RELATION_IS_OTHER_TEMP(childrel))
3315				{
3316					table_close(childrel, lockmode);
3317					continue;
3318				}
3319
3320				TargetPrivilegesCheck(childrel, ACL_TRUNCATE);
3321				rels = lappend(rels, childrel);
3322				part_rels = lappend(part_rels, childrel);
3323				relids = lappend_oid(relids, childrelid);
3324				/* Log this relation only if needed for logical decoding */
3325				if (RelationIsLogicallyLogged(childrel))
3326					relids_logged = lappend_oid(relids_logged, childrelid);
3327			}
3328		}
3329	}
3330
3331	/*
3332	 * Even if we used CASCADE on the upstream primary we explicitly default
3333	 * to replaying changes without further cascading. This might be later
3334	 * changeable with a user specified option.
3335	 *
3336	 * MySubscription->runasowner tells us whether we want to execute
3337	 * replication actions as the subscription owner; the last argument to
3338	 * TruncateGuts tells it whether we want to switch to the table owner.
3339	 * Those are exactly opposite conditions.
3340	 */
3341	ExecuteTruncateGuts(rels,
3342						relids,
3343						relids_logged,
3344						DROP_RESTRICT,
3345						restart_seqs,
3346						!MySubscription->runasowner);
3347	foreach(lc, remote_rels)
3348	{
3349		LogicalRepRelMapEntry *rel = lfirst(lc);
3350
3351		logicalrep_rel_close(rel, NoLock);
3352	}
3353	foreach(lc, part_rels)
3354	{
3355		Relation	rel = lfirst(lc);
3356
3357		table_close(rel, NoLock);
3358	}
3359
3360	end_replication_step();
3361}
3362
3363
3364/*
3365 * Logical replication protocol message dispatcher.
3366 */
3367void
3368apply_dispatch(StringInfo s)
3369{
3370	LogicalRepMsgType action = pq_getmsgbyte(s);
3371	LogicalRepMsgType saved_command;
3372
3373	/*
3374	 * Set the current command being applied. Since this function can be
3375	 * called recursively when applying spooled changes, save the current
3376	 * command.
3377	 */
3378	saved_command = apply_error_callback_arg.command;
3379	apply_error_callback_arg.command = action;
3380
3381	switch (action)
3382	{
3383		case LOGICAL_REP_MSG_BEGIN:
3384			apply_handle_begin(s);
3385			break;
3386
3387		case LOGICAL_REP_MSG_COMMIT:
3388			apply_handle_commit(s);
3389			break;
3390
3391		case LOGICAL_REP_MSG_INSERT:
3392			apply_handle_insert(s);
3393			break;
3394
3395		case LOGICAL_REP_MSG_UPDATE:
3396			apply_handle_update(s);
3397			break;
3398
3399		case LOGICAL_REP_MSG_DELETE:
3400			apply_handle_delete(s);
3401			break;
3402
3403		case LOGICAL_REP_MSG_TRUNCATE:
3404			apply_handle_truncate(s);
3405			break;
3406
3407		case LOGICAL_REP_MSG_RELATION:
3408			apply_handle_relation(s);
3409			break;
3410
3411		case LOGICAL_REP_MSG_TYPE:
3412			apply_handle_type(s);
3413			break;
3414
3415		case LOGICAL_REP_MSG_ORIGIN:
3416			apply_handle_origin(s);
3417			break;
3418
3419		case LOGICAL_REP_MSG_MESSAGE:
3420
3421			/*
3422			 * Logical replication does not use generic logical messages yet.
3423			 * Although, it could be used by other applications that use this
3424			 * output plugin.
3425			 */
3426			break;
3427
3428		case LOGICAL_REP_MSG_STREAM_START:
3429			apply_handle_stream_start(s);
3430			break;
3431
3432		case LOGICAL_REP_MSG_STREAM_STOP:
3433			apply_handle_stream_stop(s);
3434			break;
3435
3436		case LOGICAL_REP_MSG_STREAM_ABORT:
3437			apply_handle_stream_abort(s);
3438			break;
3439
3440		case LOGICAL_REP_MSG_STREAM_COMMIT:
3441			apply_handle_stream_commit(s);
3442			break;
3443
3444		case LOGICAL_REP_MSG_BEGIN_PREPARE:
3445			apply_handle_begin_prepare(s);
3446			break;
3447
3448		case LOGICAL_REP_MSG_PREPARE:
3449			apply_handle_prepare(s);
3450			break;
3451
3452		case LOGICAL_REP_MSG_COMMIT_PREPARED:
3453			apply_handle_commit_prepared(s);
3454			break;
3455
3456		case LOGICAL_REP_MSG_ROLLBACK_PREPARED:
3457			apply_handle_rollback_prepared(s);
3458			break;
3459
3460		case LOGICAL_REP_MSG_STREAM_PREPARE:
3461			apply_handle_stream_prepare(s);
3462			break;
3463
3464		default:
3465			ereport(ERROR,
3466					(errcode(ERRCODE_PROTOCOL_VIOLATION),
3467					 errmsg("invalid logical replication message type \"??? (%d)\"", action)));
3468	}
3469
3470	/* Reset the current command */
3471	apply_error_callback_arg.command = saved_command;
3472}
3473
3474/*
3475 * Figure out which write/flush positions to report to the walsender process.
3476 *
3477 * We can't simply report back the last LSN the walsender sent us because the
3478 * local transaction might not yet be flushed to disk locally. Instead we
3479 * build a list that associates local with remote LSNs for every commit. When
3480 * reporting back the flush position to the sender we iterate that list and
3481 * check which entries on it are already locally flushed. Those we can report
3482 * as having been flushed.
3483 *
3484 * The have_pending_txes is true if there are outstanding transactions that
3485 * need to be flushed.
3486 */
3487static void
3488get_flush_position(XLogRecPtr *write, XLogRecPtr *flush,
3489				   bool *have_pending_txes)
3490{
3491	dlist_mutable_iter iter;
3492	XLogRecPtr	local_flush = GetFlushRecPtr(NULL);
3493
3494	*write = InvalidXLogRecPtr;
3495	*flush = InvalidXLogRecPtr;
3496
3497	dlist_foreach_modify(iter, &lsn_mapping)
3498	{
3499		FlushPosition *pos =
3500			dlist_container(FlushPosition, node, iter.cur);
3501
3502		*write = pos->remote_end;
3503
3504		if (pos->local_end <= local_flush)
3505		{
3506			*flush = pos->remote_end;
3507			dlist_delete(iter.cur);
3508			pfree(pos);
3509		}
3510		else
3511		{
3512			/*
3513			 * Don't want to uselessly iterate over the rest of the list which
3514			 * could potentially be long. Instead get the last element and
3515			 * grab the write position from there.
3516			 */
3517			pos = dlist_tail_element(FlushPosition, node,
3518									 &lsn_mapping);
3519			*write = pos->remote_end;
3520			*have_pending_txes = true;
3521			return;
3522		}
3523	}
3524
3525	*have_pending_txes = !dlist_is_empty(&lsn_mapping);
3526}
3527
3528/*
3529 * Store current remote/local lsn pair in the tracking list.
3530 */
3531void
3532store_flush_position(XLogRecPtr remote_lsn, XLogRecPtr local_lsn)
3533{
3534	FlushPosition *flushpos;
3535
3536	/*
3537	 * Skip for parallel apply workers, because the lsn_mapping is maintained
3538	 * by the leader apply worker.
3539	 */
3540	if (am_parallel_apply_worker())
3541		return;
3542
3543	/* Need to do this in permanent context */
3544	MemoryContextSwitchTo(ApplyContext);
3545
3546	/* Track commit lsn  */
3547	flushpos = (FlushPosition *) palloc(sizeof(FlushPosition));
3548	flushpos->local_end = local_lsn;
3549	flushpos->remote_end = remote_lsn;
3550
3551	dlist_push_tail(&lsn_mapping, &flushpos->node);
3552	MemoryContextSwitchTo(ApplyMessageContext);
3553}
3554
3555
3556/* Update statistics of the worker. */
3557static void
3558UpdateWorkerStats(XLogRecPtr last_lsn, TimestampTz send_time, bool reply)
3559{
3560	MyLogicalRepWorker->last_lsn = last_lsn;
3561	MyLogicalRepWorker->last_send_time = send_time;
3562	MyLogicalRepWorker->last_recv_time = GetCurrentTimestamp();
3563	if (reply)
3564	{
3565		MyLogicalRepWorker->reply_lsn = last_lsn;
3566		MyLogicalRepWorker->reply_time = send_time;
3567	}
3568}
3569
3570/*
3571 * Apply main loop.
3572 */
3573static void
3574LogicalRepApplyLoop(XLogRecPtr last_received)
3575{
3576	TimestampTz last_recv_timestamp = GetCurrentTimestamp();
3577	bool		ping_sent = false;
3578	TimeLineID	tli;
3579	ErrorContextCallback errcallback;
3580
3581	/*
3582	 * Init the ApplyMessageContext which we clean up after each replication
3583	 * protocol message.
3584	 */
3585	ApplyMessageContext = AllocSetContextCreate(ApplyContext,
3586												"ApplyMessageContext",
3587												ALLOCSET_DEFAULT_SIZES);
3588
3589	/*
3590	 * This memory context is used for per-stream data when the streaming mode
3591	 * is enabled. This context is reset on each stream stop.
3592	 */
3593	LogicalStreamingContext = AllocSetContextCreate(ApplyContext,
3594													"LogicalStreamingContext",
3595													ALLOCSET_DEFAULT_SIZES);
3596
3597	/* mark as idle, before starting to loop */
3598	pgstat_report_activity(STATE_IDLE, NULL);
3599
3600	/*
3601	 * Push apply error context callback. Fields will be filled while applying
3602	 * a change.
3603	 */
3604	errcallback.callback = apply_error_callback;
3605	errcallback.previous = error_context_stack;
3606	error_context_stack = &errcallback;
3607	apply_error_context_stack = error_context_stack;
3608
3609	/* This outer loop iterates once per wait. */
3610	for (;;)
3611	{
3612		pgsocket	fd = PGINVALID_SOCKET;
3613		int			rc;
3614		int			len;
3615		char	   *buf = NULL;
3616		bool		endofstream = false;
3617		long		wait_time;
3618
3619		CHECK_FOR_INTERRUPTS();
3620
3621		MemoryContextSwitchTo(ApplyMessageContext);
3622
3623		len = walrcv_receive(LogRepWorkerWalRcvConn, &buf, &fd);
3624
3625		if (len != 0)
3626		{
3627			/* Loop to process all available data (without blocking). */
3628			for (;;)
3629			{
3630				CHECK_FOR_INTERRUPTS();
3631
3632				if (len == 0)
3633				{
3634					break;
3635				}
3636				else if (len < 0)
3637				{
3638					ereport(LOG,
3639							(errmsg("data stream from publisher has ended")));
3640					endofstream = true;
3641					break;
3642				}
3643				else
3644				{
3645					int			c;
3646					StringInfoData s;
3647
3648					if (ConfigReloadPending)
3649					{
3650						ConfigReloadPending = false;
3651						ProcessConfigFile(PGC_SIGHUP);
3652					}
3653
3654					/* Reset timeout. */
3655					last_recv_timestamp = GetCurrentTimestamp();
3656					ping_sent = false;
3657
3658					/* Ensure we are reading the data into our memory context. */
3659					MemoryContextSwitchTo(ApplyMessageContext);
3660
3661					initReadOnlyStringInfo(&s, buf, len);
3662
3663					c = pq_getmsgbyte(&s);
3664
3665					if (c == 'w')
3666					{
3667						XLogRecPtr	start_lsn;
3668						XLogRecPtr	end_lsn;
3669						TimestampTz send_time;
3670
3671						start_lsn = pq_getmsgint64(&s);
3672						end_lsn = pq_getmsgint64(&s);
3673						send_time = pq_getmsgint64(&s);
3674
3675						if (last_received < start_lsn)
3676							last_received = start_lsn;
3677
3678						if (last_received < end_lsn)
3679							last_received = end_lsn;
3680
3681						UpdateWorkerStats(last_received, send_time, false);
3682
3683						apply_dispatch(&s);
3684					}
3685					else if (c == 'k')
3686					{
3687						XLogRecPtr	end_lsn;
3688						TimestampTz timestamp;
3689						bool		reply_requested;
3690
3691						end_lsn = pq_getmsgint64(&s);
3692						timestamp = pq_getmsgint64(&s);
3693						reply_requested = pq_getmsgbyte(&s);
3694
3695						if (last_received < end_lsn)
3696							last_received = end_lsn;
3697
3698						send_feedback(last_received, reply_requested, false);
3699						UpdateWorkerStats(last_received, timestamp, true);
3700					}
3701					/* other message types are purposefully ignored */
3702
3703					MemoryContextReset(ApplyMessageContext);
3704				}
3705
3706				len = walrcv_receive(LogRepWorkerWalRcvConn, &buf, &fd);
3707			}
3708		}
3709
3710		/* confirm all writes so far */
3711		send_feedback(last_received, false, false);
3712
3713		if (!in_remote_transaction && !in_streamed_transaction)
3714		{
3715			/*
3716			 * If we didn't get any transactions for a while there might be
3717			 * unconsumed invalidation messages in the queue, consume them
3718			 * now.
3719			 */
3720			AcceptInvalidationMessages();
3721			maybe_reread_subscription();
3722
3723			/* Process any table synchronization changes. */
3724			process_syncing_tables(last_received);
3725		}
3726
3727		/* Cleanup the memory. */
3728		MemoryContextReset(ApplyMessageContext);
3729		MemoryContextSwitchTo(TopMemoryContext);
3730
3731		/* Check if we need to exit the streaming loop. */
3732		if (endofstream)
3733			break;
3734
3735		/*
3736		 * Wait for more data or latch.  If we have unflushed transactions,
3737		 * wake up after WalWriterDelay to see if they've been flushed yet (in
3738		 * which case we should send a feedback message).  Otherwise, there's
3739		 * no particular urgency about waking up unless we get data or a
3740		 * signal.
3741		 */
3742		if (!dlist_is_empty(&lsn_mapping))
3743			wait_time = WalWriterDelay;
3744		else
3745			wait_time = NAPTIME_PER_CYCLE;
3746
3747		rc = WaitLatchOrSocket(MyLatch,
3748							   WL_SOCKET_READABLE | WL_LATCH_SET |
3749							   WL_TIMEOUT | WL_EXIT_ON_PM_DEATH,
3750							   fd, wait_time,
3751							   WAIT_EVENT_LOGICAL_APPLY_MAIN);
3752
3753		if (rc & WL_LATCH_SET)
3754		{
3755			ResetLatch(MyLatch);
3756			CHECK_FOR_INTERRUPTS();
3757		}
3758
3759		if (ConfigReloadPending)
3760		{
3761			ConfigReloadPending = false;
3762			ProcessConfigFile(PGC_SIGHUP);
3763		}
3764
3765		if (rc & WL_TIMEOUT)
3766		{
3767			/*
3768			 * We didn't receive anything new. If we haven't heard anything
3769			 * from the server for more than wal_receiver_timeout / 2, ping
3770			 * the server. Also, if it's been longer than
3771			 * wal_receiver_status_interval since the last update we sent,
3772			 * send a status update to the primary anyway, to report any
3773			 * progress in applying WAL.
3774			 */
3775			bool		requestReply = false;
3776
3777			/*
3778			 * Check if time since last receive from primary has reached the
3779			 * configured limit.
3780			 */
3781			if (wal_receiver_timeout > 0)
3782			{
3783				TimestampTz now = GetCurrentTimestamp();
3784				TimestampTz timeout;
3785
3786				timeout =
3787					TimestampTzPlusMilliseconds(last_recv_timestamp,
3788												wal_receiver_timeout);
3789
3790				if (now >= timeout)
3791					ereport(ERROR,
3792							(errcode(ERRCODE_CONNECTION_FAILURE),
3793							 errmsg("terminating logical replication worker due to timeout")));
3794
3795				/* Check to see if it's time for a ping. */
3796				if (!ping_sent)
3797				{
3798					timeout = TimestampTzPlusMilliseconds(last_recv_timestamp,
3799														  (wal_receiver_timeout / 2));
3800					if (now >= timeout)
3801					{
3802						requestReply = true;
3803						ping_sent = true;
3804					}
3805				}
3806			}
3807
3808			send_feedback(last_received, requestReply, requestReply);
3809
3810			/*
3811			 * Force reporting to ensure long idle periods don't lead to
3812			 * arbitrarily delayed stats. Stats can only be reported outside
3813			 * of (implicit or explicit) transactions. That shouldn't lead to
3814			 * stats being delayed for long, because transactions are either
3815			 * sent as a whole on commit or streamed. Streamed transactions
3816			 * are spilled to disk and applied on commit.
3817			 */
3818			if (!IsTransactionState())
3819				pgstat_report_stat(true);
3820		}
3821	}
3822
3823	/* Pop the error context stack */
3824	error_context_stack = errcallback.previous;
3825	apply_error_context_stack = error_context_stack;
3826
3827	/* All done */
3828	walrcv_endstreaming(LogRepWorkerWalRcvConn, &tli);
3829}
3830
3831/*
3832 * Send a Standby Status Update message to server.
3833 *
3834 * 'recvpos' is the latest LSN we've received data to, force is set if we need
3835 * to send a response to avoid timeouts.
3836 */
3837static void
3838send_feedback(XLogRecPtr recvpos, bool force, bool requestReply)
3839{
3840	static StringInfo reply_message = NULL;
3841	static TimestampTz send_time = 0;
3842
3843	static XLogRecPtr last_recvpos = InvalidXLogRecPtr;
3844	static XLogRecPtr last_writepos = InvalidXLogRecPtr;
3845	static XLogRecPtr last_flushpos = InvalidXLogRecPtr;
3846
3847	XLogRecPtr	writepos;
3848	XLogRecPtr	flushpos;
3849	TimestampTz now;
3850	bool		have_pending_txes;
3851
3852	/*
3853	 * If the user doesn't want status to be reported to the publisher, be
3854	 * sure to exit before doing anything at all.
3855	 */
3856	if (!force && wal_receiver_status_interval <= 0)
3857		return;
3858
3859	/* It's legal to not pass a recvpos */
3860	if (recvpos < last_recvpos)
3861		recvpos = last_recvpos;
3862
3863	get_flush_position(&writepos, &flushpos, &have_pending_txes);
3864
3865	/*
3866	 * No outstanding transactions to flush, we can report the latest received
3867	 * position. This is important for synchronous replication.
3868	 */
3869	if (!have_pending_txes)
3870		flushpos = writepos = recvpos;
3871
3872	if (writepos < last_writepos)
3873		writepos = last_writepos;
3874
3875	if (flushpos < last_flushpos)
3876		flushpos = last_flushpos;
3877
3878	now = GetCurrentTimestamp();
3879
3880	/* if we've already reported everything we're good */
3881	if (!force &&
3882		writepos == last_writepos &&
3883		flushpos == last_flushpos &&
3884		!TimestampDifferenceExceeds(send_time, now,
3885									wal_receiver_status_interval * 1000))
3886		return;
3887	send_time = now;
3888
3889	if (!reply_message)
3890	{
3891		MemoryContext oldctx = MemoryContextSwitchTo(ApplyContext);
3892
3893		reply_message = makeStringInfo();
3894		MemoryContextSwitchTo(oldctx);
3895	}
3896	else
3897		resetStringInfo(reply_message);
3898
3899	pq_sendbyte(reply_message, 'r');
3900	pq_sendint64(reply_message, recvpos);	/* write */
3901	pq_sendint64(reply_message, flushpos);	/* flush */
3902	pq_sendint64(reply_message, writepos);	/* apply */
3903	pq_sendint64(reply_message, now);	/* sendTime */
3904	pq_sendbyte(reply_message, requestReply);	/* replyRequested */
3905
3906	elog(DEBUG2, "sending feedback (force %d) to recv %X/%X, write %X/%X, flush %X/%X",
3907		 force,
3908		 LSN_FORMAT_ARGS(recvpos),
3909		 LSN_FORMAT_ARGS(writepos),
3910		 LSN_FORMAT_ARGS(flushpos));
3911
3912	walrcv_send(LogRepWorkerWalRcvConn,
3913				reply_message->data, reply_message->len);
3914
3915	if (recvpos > last_recvpos)
3916		last_recvpos = recvpos;
3917	if (writepos > last_writepos)
3918		last_writepos = writepos;
3919	if (flushpos > last_flushpos)
3920		last_flushpos = flushpos;
3921}
3922
3923/*
3924 * Exit routine for apply workers due to subscription parameter changes.
3925 */
3926static void
3927apply_worker_exit(void)
3928{
3929	if (am_parallel_apply_worker())
3930	{
3931		/*
3932		 * Don't stop the parallel apply worker as the leader will detect the
3933		 * subscription parameter change and restart logical replication later
3934		 * anyway. This also prevents the leader from reporting errors when
3935		 * trying to communicate with a stopped parallel apply worker, which
3936		 * would accidentally disable subscriptions if disable_on_error was
3937		 * set.
3938		 */
3939		return;
3940	}
3941
3942	/*
3943	 * Reset the last-start time for this apply worker so that the launcher
3944	 * will restart it without waiting for wal_retrieve_retry_interval if the
3945	 * subscription is still active, and so that we won't leak that hash table
3946	 * entry if it isn't.
3947	 */
3948	if (am_leader_apply_worker())
3949		ApplyLauncherForgetWorkerStartTime(MyLogicalRepWorker->subid);
3950
3951	proc_exit(0);
3952}
3953
3954/*
3955 * Reread subscription info if needed.
3956 *
3957 * For significant changes, we react by exiting the current process; a new
3958 * one will be launched afterwards if needed.
3959 */
3960void
3961maybe_reread_subscription(void)
3962{
3963	MemoryContext oldctx;
3964	Subscription *newsub;
3965	bool		started_tx = false;
3966
3967	/* When cache state is valid there is nothing to do here. */
3968	if (MySubscriptionValid)
3969		return;
3970
3971	/* This function might be called inside or outside of transaction. */
3972	if (!IsTransactionState())
3973	{
3974		StartTransactionCommand();
3975		started_tx = true;
3976	}
3977
3978	/* Ensure allocations in permanent context. */
3979	oldctx = MemoryContextSwitchTo(ApplyContext);
3980
3981	newsub = GetSubscription(MyLogicalRepWorker->subid, true);
3982
3983	/*
3984	 * Exit if the subscription was removed. This normally should not happen
3985	 * as the worker gets killed during DROP SUBSCRIPTION.
3986	 */
3987	if (!newsub)
3988	{
3989		ereport(LOG,
3990				(errmsg("logical replication worker for subscription \"%s\" will stop because the subscription was removed",
3991						MySubscription->name)));
3992
3993		/* Ensure we remove no-longer-useful entry for worker's start time */
3994		if (am_leader_apply_worker())
3995			ApplyLauncherForgetWorkerStartTime(MyLogicalRepWorker->subid);
3996
3997		proc_exit(0);
3998	}
3999
4000	/* Exit if the subscription was disabled. */
4001	if (!newsub->enabled)
4002	{
4003		ereport(LOG,
4004				(errmsg("logical replication worker for subscription \"%s\" will stop because the subscription was disabled",
4005						MySubscription->name)));
4006
4007		apply_worker_exit();
4008	}
4009
4010	/* !slotname should never happen when enabled is true. */
4011	Assert(newsub->slotname);
4012
4013	/* two-phase cannot be altered while the worker is running */
4014	Assert(newsub->twophasestate == MySubscription->twophasestate);
4015
4016	/*
4017	 * Exit if any parameter that affects the remote connection was changed.
4018	 * The launcher will start a new worker but note that the parallel apply
4019	 * worker won't restart if the streaming option's value is changed from
4020	 * 'parallel' to any other value or the server decides not to stream the
4021	 * in-progress transaction.
4022	 */
4023	if (strcmp(newsub->conninfo, MySubscription->conninfo) != 0 ||
4024		strcmp(newsub->name, MySubscription->name) != 0 ||
4025		strcmp(newsub->slotname, MySubscription->slotname) != 0 ||
4026		newsub->binary != MySubscription->binary ||
4027		newsub->stream != MySubscription->stream ||
4028		newsub->passwordrequired != MySubscription->passwordrequired ||
4029		strcmp(newsub->origin, MySubscription->origin) != 0 ||
4030		newsub->owner != MySubscription->owner ||
4031		!equal(newsub->publications, MySubscription->publications))
4032	{
4033		if (am_parallel_apply_worker())
4034			ereport(LOG,
4035					(errmsg("logical replication parallel apply worker for subscription \"%s\" will stop because of a parameter change",
4036							MySubscription->name)));
4037		else
4038			ereport(LOG,
4039					(errmsg("logical replication worker for subscription \"%s\" will restart because of a parameter change",
4040							MySubscription->name)));
4041
4042		apply_worker_exit();
4043	}
4044
4045	/*
4046	 * Exit if the subscription owner's superuser privileges have been
4047	 * revoked.
4048	 */
4049	if (!newsub->ownersuperuser && MySubscription->ownersuperuser)
4050	{
4051		if (am_parallel_apply_worker())
4052			ereport(LOG,
4053					errmsg("logical replication parallel apply worker for subscription \"%s\" will stop because the subscription owner's superuser privileges have been revoked",
4054						   MySubscription->name));
4055		else
4056			ereport(LOG,
4057					errmsg("logical replication worker for subscription \"%s\" will restart because the subscription owner's superuser privileges have been revoked",
4058						   MySubscription->name));
4059
4060		apply_worker_exit();
4061	}
4062
4063	/* Check for other changes that should never happen too. */
4064	if (newsub->dbid != MySubscription->dbid)
4065	{
4066		elog(ERROR, "subscription %u changed unexpectedly",
4067			 MyLogicalRepWorker->subid);
4068	}
4069
4070	/* Clean old subscription info and switch to new one. */
4071	FreeSubscription(MySubscription);
4072	MySubscription = newsub;
4073
4074	MemoryContextSwitchTo(oldctx);
4075
4076	/* Change synchronous commit according to the user's wishes */
4077	SetConfigOption("synchronous_commit", MySubscription->synccommit,
4078					PGC_BACKEND, PGC_S_OVERRIDE);
4079
4080	if (started_tx)
4081		CommitTransactionCommand();
4082
4083	MySubscriptionValid = true;
4084}
4085
4086/*
4087 * Callback from subscription syscache invalidation.
4088 */
4089static void
4090subscription_change_cb(Datum arg, int cacheid, uint32 hashvalue)
4091{
4092	MySubscriptionValid = false;
4093}
4094
4095/*
4096 * subxact_info_write
4097 *	  Store information about subxacts for a toplevel transaction.
4098 *
4099 * For each subxact we store offset of its first change in the main file.
4100 * The file is always over-written as a whole.
4101 *
4102 * XXX We should only store subxacts that were not aborted yet.
4103 */
4104static void
4105subxact_info_write(Oid subid, TransactionId xid)
4106{
4107	char		path[MAXPGPATH];
4108	Size		len;
4109	BufFile    *fd;
4110
4111	Assert(TransactionIdIsValid(xid));
4112
4113	/* construct the subxact filename */
4114	subxact_filename(path, subid, xid);
4115
4116	/* Delete the subxacts file, if exists. */
4117	if (subxact_data.nsubxacts == 0)
4118	{
4119		cleanup_subxact_info();
4120		BufFileDeleteFileSet(MyLogicalRepWorker->stream_fileset, path, true);
4121
4122		return;
4123	}
4124
4125	/*
4126	 * Create the subxact file if it not already created, otherwise open the
4127	 * existing file.
4128	 */
4129	fd = BufFileOpenFileSet(MyLogicalRepWorker->stream_fileset, path, O_RDWR,
4130							true);
4131	if (fd == NULL)
4132		fd = BufFileCreateFileSet(MyLogicalRepWorker->stream_fileset, path);
4133
4134	len = sizeof(SubXactInfo) * subxact_data.nsubxacts;
4135
4136	/* Write the subxact count and subxact info */
4137	BufFileWrite(fd, &subxact_data.nsubxacts, sizeof(subxact_data.nsubxacts));
4138	BufFileWrite(fd, subxact_data.subxacts, len);
4139
4140	BufFileClose(fd);
4141
4142	/* free the memory allocated for subxact info */
4143	cleanup_subxact_info();
4144}
4145
4146/*
4147 * subxact_info_read
4148 *	  Restore information about subxacts of a streamed transaction.
4149 *
4150 * Read information about subxacts into the structure subxact_data that can be
4151 * used later.
4152 */
4153static void
4154subxact_info_read(Oid subid, TransactionId xid)
4155{
4156	char		path[MAXPGPATH];
4157	Size		len;
4158	BufFile    *fd;
4159	MemoryContext oldctx;
4160
4161	Assert(!subxact_data.subxacts);
4162	Assert(subxact_data.nsubxacts == 0);
4163	Assert(subxact_data.nsubxacts_max == 0);
4164
4165	/*
4166	 * If the subxact file doesn't exist that means we don't have any subxact
4167	 * info.
4168	 */
4169	subxact_filename(path, subid, xid);
4170	fd = BufFileOpenFileSet(MyLogicalRepWorker->stream_fileset, path, O_RDONLY,
4171							true);
4172	if (fd == NULL)
4173		return;
4174
4175	/* read number of subxact items */
4176	BufFileReadExact(fd, &subxact_data.nsubxacts, sizeof(subxact_data.nsubxacts));
4177
4178	len = sizeof(SubXactInfo) * subxact_data.nsubxacts;
4179
4180	/* we keep the maximum as a power of 2 */
4181	subxact_data.nsubxacts_max = 1 << my_log2(subxact_data.nsubxacts);
4182
4183	/*
4184	 * Allocate subxact information in the logical streaming context. We need
4185	 * this information during the complete stream so that we can add the sub
4186	 * transaction info to this. On stream stop we will flush this information
4187	 * to the subxact file and reset the logical streaming context.
4188	 */
4189	oldctx = MemoryContextSwitchTo(LogicalStreamingContext);
4190	subxact_data.subxacts = palloc(subxact_data.nsubxacts_max *
4191								   sizeof(SubXactInfo));
4192	MemoryContextSwitchTo(oldctx);
4193
4194	if (len > 0)
4195		BufFileReadExact(fd, subxact_data.subxacts, len);
4196
4197	BufFileClose(fd);
4198}
4199
4200/*
4201 * subxact_info_add
4202 *	  Add information about a subxact (offset in the main file).
4203 */
4204static void
4205subxact_info_add(TransactionId xid)
4206{
4207	SubXactInfo *subxacts = subxact_data.subxacts;
4208	int64		i;
4209
4210	/* We must have a valid top level stream xid and a stream fd. */
4211	Assert(TransactionIdIsValid(stream_xid));
4212	Assert(stream_fd != NULL);
4213
4214	/*
4215	 * If the XID matches the toplevel transaction, we don't want to add it.
4216	 */
4217	if (stream_xid == xid)
4218		return;
4219
4220	/*
4221	 * In most cases we're checking the same subxact as we've already seen in
4222	 * the last call, so make sure to ignore it (this change comes later).
4223	 */
4224	if (subxact_data.subxact_last == xid)
4225		return;
4226
4227	/* OK, remember we're processing this XID. */
4228	subxact_data.subxact_last = xid;
4229
4230	/*
4231	 * Check if the transaction is already present in the array of subxact. We
4232	 * intentionally scan the array from the tail, because we're likely adding
4233	 * a change for the most recent subtransactions.
4234	 *
4235	 * XXX Can we rely on the subxact XIDs arriving in sorted order? That
4236	 * would allow us to use binary search here.
4237	 */
4238	for (i = subxact_data.nsubxacts; i > 0; i--)
4239	{
4240		/* found, so we're done */
4241		if (subxacts[i - 1].xid == xid)
4242			return;
4243	}
4244
4245	/* This is a new subxact, so we need to add it to the array. */
4246	if (subxact_data.nsubxacts == 0)
4247	{
4248		MemoryContext oldctx;
4249
4250		subxact_data.nsubxacts_max = 128;
4251
4252		/*
4253		 * Allocate this memory for subxacts in per-stream context, see
4254		 * subxact_info_read.
4255		 */
4256		oldctx = MemoryContextSwitchTo(LogicalStreamingContext);
4257		subxacts = palloc(subxact_data.nsubxacts_max * sizeof(SubXactInfo));
4258		MemoryContextSwitchTo(oldctx);
4259	}
4260	else if (subxact_data.nsubxacts == subxact_data.nsubxacts_max)
4261	{
4262		subxact_data.nsubxacts_max *= 2;
4263		subxacts = repalloc(subxacts,
4264							subxact_data.nsubxacts_max * sizeof(SubXactInfo));
4265	}
4266
4267	subxacts[subxact_data.nsubxacts].xid = xid;
4268
4269	/*
4270	 * Get the current offset of the stream file and store it as offset of
4271	 * this subxact.
4272	 */
4273	BufFileTell(stream_fd,
4274				&subxacts[subxact_data.nsubxacts].fileno,
4275				&subxacts[subxact_data.nsubxacts].offset);
4276
4277	subxact_data.nsubxacts++;
4278	subxact_data.subxacts = subxacts;
4279}
4280
4281/* format filename for file containing the info about subxacts */
4282static inline void
4283subxact_filename(char *path, Oid subid, TransactionId xid)
4284{
4285	snprintf(path, MAXPGPATH, "%u-%u.subxacts", subid, xid);
4286}
4287
4288/* format filename for file containing serialized changes */
4289static inline void
4290changes_filename(char *path, Oid subid, TransactionId xid)
4291{
4292	snprintf(path, MAXPGPATH, "%u-%u.changes", subid, xid);
4293}
4294
4295/*
4296 * stream_cleanup_files
4297 *	  Cleanup files for a subscription / toplevel transaction.
4298 *
4299 * Remove files with serialized changes and subxact info for a particular
4300 * toplevel transaction. Each subscription has a separate set of files
4301 * for any toplevel transaction.
4302 */
4303void
4304stream_cleanup_files(Oid subid, TransactionId xid)
4305{
4306	char		path[MAXPGPATH];
4307
4308	/* Delete the changes file. */
4309	changes_filename(path, subid, xid);
4310	BufFileDeleteFileSet(MyLogicalRepWorker->stream_fileset, path, false);
4311
4312	/* Delete the subxact file, if it exists. */
4313	subxact_filename(path, subid, xid);
4314	BufFileDeleteFileSet(MyLogicalRepWorker->stream_fileset, path, true);
4315}
4316
4317/*
4318 * stream_open_file
4319 *	  Open a file that we'll use to serialize changes for a toplevel
4320 * transaction.
4321 *
4322 * Open a file for streamed changes from a toplevel transaction identified
4323 * by stream_xid (global variable). If it's the first chunk of streamed
4324 * changes for this transaction, create the buffile, otherwise open the
4325 * previously created file.
4326 */
4327static void
4328stream_open_file(Oid subid, TransactionId xid, bool first_segment)
4329{
4330	char		path[MAXPGPATH];
4331	MemoryContext oldcxt;
4332
4333	Assert(OidIsValid(subid));
4334	Assert(TransactionIdIsValid(xid));
4335	Assert(stream_fd == NULL);
4336
4337
4338	changes_filename(path, subid, xid);
4339	elog(DEBUG1, "opening file \"%s\" for streamed changes", path);
4340
4341	/*
4342	 * Create/open the buffiles under the logical streaming context so that we
4343	 * have those files until stream stop.
4344	 */
4345	oldcxt = MemoryContextSwitchTo(LogicalStreamingContext);
4346
4347	/*
4348	 * If this is the first streamed segment, create the changes file.
4349	 * Otherwise, just open the file for writing, in append mode.
4350	 */
4351	if (first_segment)
4352		stream_fd = BufFileCreateFileSet(MyLogicalRepWorker->stream_fileset,
4353										 path);
4354	else
4355	{
4356		/*
4357		 * Open the file and seek to the end of the file because we always
4358		 * append the changes file.
4359		 */
4360		stream_fd = BufFileOpenFileSet(MyLogicalRepWorker->stream_fileset,
4361									   path, O_RDWR, false);
4362		BufFileSeek(stream_fd, 0, 0, SEEK_END);
4363	}
4364
4365	MemoryContextSwitchTo(oldcxt);
4366}
4367
4368/*
4369 * stream_close_file
4370 *	  Close the currently open file with streamed changes.
4371 */
4372static void
4373stream_close_file(void)
4374{
4375	Assert(stream_fd != NULL);
4376
4377	BufFileClose(stream_fd);
4378
4379	stream_fd = NULL;
4380}
4381
4382/*
4383 * stream_write_change
4384 *	  Serialize a change to a file for the current toplevel transaction.
4385 *
4386 * The change is serialized in a simple format, with length (not including
4387 * the length), action code (identifying the message type) and message
4388 * contents (without the subxact TransactionId value).
4389 */
4390static void
4391stream_write_change(char action, StringInfo s)
4392{
4393	int			len;
4394
4395	Assert(stream_fd != NULL);
4396
4397	/* total on-disk size, including the action type character */
4398	len = (s->len - s->cursor) + sizeof(char);
4399
4400	/* first write the size */
4401	BufFileWrite(stream_fd, &len, sizeof(len));
4402
4403	/* then the action */
4404	BufFileWrite(stream_fd, &action, sizeof(action));
4405
4406	/* and finally the remaining part of the buffer (after the XID) */
4407	len = (s->len - s->cursor);
4408
4409	BufFileWrite(stream_fd, &s->data[s->cursor], len);
4410}
4411
4412/*
4413 * stream_open_and_write_change
4414 *	  Serialize a message to a file for the given transaction.
4415 *
4416 * This function is similar to stream_write_change except that it will open the
4417 * target file if not already before writing the message and close the file at
4418 * the end.
4419 */
4420static void
4421stream_open_and_write_change(TransactionId xid, char action, StringInfo s)
4422{
4423	Assert(!in_streamed_transaction);
4424
4425	if (!stream_fd)
4426		stream_start_internal(xid, false);
4427
4428	stream_write_change(action, s);
4429	stream_stop_internal(xid);
4430}
4431
4432/*
4433 * Sets streaming options including replication slot name and origin start
4434 * position. Workers need these options for logical replication.
4435 */
4436void
4437set_stream_options(WalRcvStreamOptions *options,
4438				   char *slotname,
4439				   XLogRecPtr *origin_startpos)
4440{
4441	int			server_version;
4442
4443	options->logical = true;
4444	options->startpoint = *origin_startpos;
4445	options->slotname = slotname;
4446
4447	server_version = walrcv_server_version(LogRepWorkerWalRcvConn);
4448	options->proto.logical.proto_version =
4449		server_version >= 160000 ? LOGICALREP_PROTO_STREAM_PARALLEL_VERSION_NUM :
4450		server_version >= 150000 ? LOGICALREP_PROTO_TWOPHASE_VERSION_NUM :
4451		server_version >= 140000 ? LOGICALREP_PROTO_STREAM_VERSION_NUM :
4452		LOGICALREP_PROTO_VERSION_NUM;
4453
4454	options->proto.logical.publication_names = MySubscription->publications;
4455	options->proto.logical.binary = MySubscription->binary;
4456
4457	/*
4458	 * Assign the appropriate option value for streaming option according to
4459	 * the 'streaming' mode and the publisher's ability to support that mode.
4460	 */
4461	if (server_version >= 160000 &&
4462		MySubscription->stream == LOGICALREP_STREAM_PARALLEL)
4463	{
4464		options->proto.logical.streaming_str = "parallel";
4465		MyLogicalRepWorker->parallel_apply = true;
4466	}
4467	else if (server_version >= 140000 &&
4468			 MySubscription->stream != LOGICALREP_STREAM_OFF)
4469	{
4470		options->proto.logical.streaming_str = "on";
4471		MyLogicalRepWorker->parallel_apply = false;
4472	}
4473	else
4474	{
4475		options->proto.logical.streaming_str = NULL;
4476		MyLogicalRepWorker->parallel_apply = false;
4477	}
4478
4479	options->proto.logical.twophase = false;
4480	options->proto.logical.origin = pstrdup(MySubscription->origin);
4481}
4482
4483/*
4484 * Cleanup the memory for subxacts and reset the related variables.
4485 */
4486static inline void
4487cleanup_subxact_info()
4488{
4489	if (subxact_data.subxacts)
4490		pfree(subxact_data.subxacts);
4491
4492	subxact_data.subxacts = NULL;
4493	subxact_data.subxact_last = InvalidTransactionId;
4494	subxact_data.nsubxacts = 0;
4495	subxact_data.nsubxacts_max = 0;
4496}
4497
4498/*
4499 * Common function to run the apply loop with error handling. Disable the
4500 * subscription, if necessary.
4501 *
4502 * Note that we don't handle FATAL errors which are probably because
4503 * of system resource error and are not repeatable.
4504 */
4505void
4506start_apply(XLogRecPtr origin_startpos)
4507{
4508	PG_TRY();
4509	{
4510		LogicalRepApplyLoop(origin_startpos);
4511	}
4512	PG_CATCH();
4513	{
4514		/*
4515		 * Reset the origin state to prevent the advancement of origin
4516		 * progress if we fail to apply. Otherwise, this will result in
4517		 * transaction loss as that transaction won't be sent again by the
4518		 * server.
4519		 */
4520		replorigin_reset(0, (Datum) 0);
4521
4522		if (MySubscription->disableonerr)
4523			DisableSubscriptionAndExit();
4524		else
4525		{
4526			/*
4527			 * Report the worker failed while applying changes. Abort the
4528			 * current transaction so that the stats message is sent in an
4529			 * idle state.
4530			 */
4531			AbortOutOfAnyTransaction();
4532			pgstat_report_subscription_error(MySubscription->oid, !am_tablesync_worker());
4533
4534			PG_RE_THROW();
4535		}
4536	}
4537	PG_END_TRY();
4538}
4539
4540/*
4541 * Runs the leader apply worker.
4542 *
4543 * It sets up replication origin, streaming options and then starts streaming.
4544 */
4545static void
4546run_apply_worker()
4547{
4548	char		originname[NAMEDATALEN];
4549	XLogRecPtr	origin_startpos = InvalidXLogRecPtr;
4550	char	   *slotname = NULL;
4551	WalRcvStreamOptions options;
4552	RepOriginId originid;
4553	TimeLineID	startpointTLI;
4554	char	   *err;
4555	bool		must_use_password;
4556
4557	slotname = MySubscription->slotname;
4558
4559	/*
4560	 * This shouldn't happen if the subscription is enabled, but guard against
4561	 * DDL bugs or manual catalog changes.  (libpqwalreceiver will crash if
4562	 * slot is NULL.)
4563	 */
4564	if (!slotname)
4565		ereport(ERROR,
4566				(errcode(ERRCODE_OBJECT_NOT_IN_PREREQUISITE_STATE),
4567				 errmsg("subscription has no replication slot set")));
4568
4569	/* Setup replication origin tracking. */
4570	ReplicationOriginNameForLogicalRep(MySubscription->oid, InvalidOid,
4571									   originname, sizeof(originname));
4572	StartTransactionCommand();
4573	originid = replorigin_by_name(originname, true);
4574	if (!OidIsValid(originid))
4575		originid = replorigin_create(originname);
4576	replorigin_session_setup(originid, 0);
4577	replorigin_session_origin = originid;
4578	origin_startpos = replorigin_session_get_progress(false);
4579	CommitTransactionCommand();
4580
4581	/* Is the use of a password mandatory? */
4582	must_use_password = MySubscription->passwordrequired &&
4583		!MySubscription->ownersuperuser;
4584
4585	LogRepWorkerWalRcvConn = walrcv_connect(MySubscription->conninfo, true,
4586											true, must_use_password,
4587											MySubscription->name, &err);
4588
4589	if (LogRepWorkerWalRcvConn == NULL)
4590		ereport(ERROR,
4591				(errcode(ERRCODE_CONNECTION_FAILURE),
4592				 errmsg("apply worker for subscription \"%s\" could not connect to the publisher: %s",
4593						MySubscription->name, err)));
4594
4595	/*
4596	 * We don't really use the output identify_system for anything but it does
4597	 * some initializations on the upstream so let's still call it.
4598	 */
4599	(void) walrcv_identify_system(LogRepWorkerWalRcvConn, &startpointTLI);
4600
4601	set_apply_error_context_origin(originname);
4602
4603	set_stream_options(&options, slotname, &origin_startpos);
4604
4605	/*
4606	 * Even when the two_phase mode is requested by the user, it remains as
4607	 * the tri-state PENDING until all tablesyncs have reached READY state.
4608	 * Only then, can it become ENABLED.
4609	 *
4610	 * Note: If the subscription has no tables then leave the state as
4611	 * PENDING, which allows ALTER SUBSCRIPTION ... REFRESH PUBLICATION to
4612	 * work.
4613	 */
4614	if (MySubscription->twophasestate == LOGICALREP_TWOPHASE_STATE_PENDING &&
4615		AllTablesyncsReady())
4616	{
4617		/* Start streaming with two_phase enabled */
4618		options.proto.logical.twophase = true;
4619		walrcv_startstreaming(LogRepWorkerWalRcvConn, &options);
4620
4621		StartTransactionCommand();
4622
4623		/*
4624		 * Updating pg_subscription might involve TOAST table access, so
4625		 * ensure we have a valid snapshot.
4626		 */
4627		PushActiveSnapshot(GetTransactionSnapshot());
4628
4629		UpdateTwoPhaseState(MySubscription->oid, LOGICALREP_TWOPHASE_STATE_ENABLED);
4630		MySubscription->twophasestate = LOGICALREP_TWOPHASE_STATE_ENABLED;
4631		PopActiveSnapshot();
4632		CommitTransactionCommand();
4633	}
4634	else
4635	{
4636		walrcv_startstreaming(LogRepWorkerWalRcvConn, &options);
4637	}
4638
4639	ereport(DEBUG1,
4640			(errmsg_internal("logical replication apply worker for subscription \"%s\" two_phase is %s",
4641							 MySubscription->name,
4642							 MySubscription->twophasestate == LOGICALREP_TWOPHASE_STATE_DISABLED ? "DISABLED" :
4643							 MySubscription->twophasestate == LOGICALREP_TWOPHASE_STATE_PENDING ? "PENDING" :
4644							 MySubscription->twophasestate == LOGICALREP_TWOPHASE_STATE_ENABLED ? "ENABLED" :
4645							 "?")));
4646
4647	/* Run the main loop. */
4648	start_apply(origin_startpos);
4649}
4650
4651/*
4652 * Common initialization for leader apply worker, parallel apply worker and
4653 * tablesync worker.
4654 *
4655 * Initialize the database connection, in-memory subscription and necessary
4656 * config options.
4657 */
4658void
4659InitializeLogRepWorker(void)
4660{
4661	MemoryContext oldctx;
4662
4663	/* Run as replica session replication role. */
4664	SetConfigOption("session_replication_role", "replica",
4665					PGC_SUSET, PGC_S_OVERRIDE);
4666
4667	/* Connect to our database. */
4668	BackgroundWorkerInitializeConnectionByOid(MyLogicalRepWorker->dbid,
4669											  MyLogicalRepWorker->userid,
4670											  0);
4671
4672	/*
4673	 * Set always-secure search path, so malicious users can't redirect user
4674	 * code (e.g. pg_index.indexprs).
4675	 */
4676	SetConfigOption("search_path", "", PGC_SUSET, PGC_S_OVERRIDE);
4677
4678	/* Load the subscription into persistent memory context. */
4679	ApplyContext = AllocSetContextCreate(TopMemoryContext,
4680										 "ApplyContext",
4681										 ALLOCSET_DEFAULT_SIZES);
4682	StartTransactionCommand();
4683	oldctx = MemoryContextSwitchTo(ApplyContext);
4684
4685	/*
4686	 * Lock the subscription to prevent it from being concurrently dropped,
4687	 * then re-verify its existence. After the initialization, the worker will
4688	 * be terminated gracefully if the subscription is dropped.
4689	 */
4690	LockSharedObject(SubscriptionRelationId, MyLogicalRepWorker->subid, 0,
4691					 AccessShareLock);
4692	MySubscription = GetSubscription(MyLogicalRepWorker->subid, true);
4693	if (!MySubscription)
4694	{
4695		ereport(LOG,
4696				(errmsg("logical replication worker for subscription %u will not start because the subscription was removed during startup",
4697						MyLogicalRepWorker->subid)));
4698
4699		/* Ensure we remove no-longer-useful entry for worker's start time */
4700		if (am_leader_apply_worker())
4701			ApplyLauncherForgetWorkerStartTime(MyLogicalRepWorker->subid);
4702
4703		proc_exit(0);
4704	}
4705
4706	MySubscriptionValid = true;
4707	MemoryContextSwitchTo(oldctx);
4708
4709	if (!MySubscription->enabled)
4710	{
4711		ereport(LOG,
4712				(errmsg("logical replication worker for subscription \"%s\" will not start because the subscription was disabled during startup",
4713						MySubscription->name)));
4714
4715		apply_worker_exit();
4716	}
4717
4718	/* Setup synchronous commit according to the user's wishes */
4719	SetConfigOption("synchronous_commit", MySubscription->synccommit,
4720					PGC_BACKEND, PGC_S_OVERRIDE);
4721
4722	/*
4723	 * Keep us informed about subscription or role changes. Note that the
4724	 * role's superuser privilege can be revoked.
4725	 */
4726	CacheRegisterSyscacheCallback(SUBSCRIPTIONOID,
4727								  subscription_change_cb,
4728								  (Datum) 0);
4729
4730	CacheRegisterSyscacheCallback(AUTHOID,
4731								  subscription_change_cb,
4732								  (Datum) 0);
4733
4734	if (am_tablesync_worker())
4735		ereport(LOG,
4736				(errmsg("logical replication table synchronization worker for subscription \"%s\", table \"%s\" has started",
4737						MySubscription->name,
4738						get_rel_name(MyLogicalRepWorker->relid))));
4739	else
4740		ereport(LOG,
4741				(errmsg("logical replication apply worker for subscription \"%s\" has started",
4742						MySubscription->name)));
4743
4744	CommitTransactionCommand();
4745
4746	/*
4747	 * Register a callback to reset the origin state before aborting any
4748	 * pending transaction during shutdown (see ShutdownPostgres()). This will
4749	 * avoid origin advancement for an incomplete transaction which could
4750	 * otherwise lead to its loss as such a transaction won't be sent by the
4751	 * server again.
4752	 *
4753	 * Note that even a LOG or DEBUG statement placed after setting the origin
4754	 * state may process a shutdown signal before committing the current apply
4755	 * operation. So, it is important to register such a callback here.
4756	 *
4757	 * Register this callback here to ensure that all types of logical
4758	 * replication workers that set up origins and apply remote transactions
4759	 * are protected.
4760	 */
4761	before_shmem_exit(replorigin_reset, (Datum) 0);
4762}
4763
4764/*
4765 * Reset the origin state.
4766 */
4767static void
4768replorigin_reset(int code, Datum arg)
4769{
4770	replorigin_session_origin = InvalidRepOriginId;
4771	replorigin_session_origin_lsn = InvalidXLogRecPtr;
4772	replorigin_session_origin_timestamp = 0;
4773}
4774
4775/* Common function to setup the leader apply or tablesync worker. */
4776void
4777SetupApplyOrSyncWorker(int worker_slot)
4778{
4779	/* Attach to slot */
4780	logicalrep_worker_attach(worker_slot);
4781
4782	Assert(am_tablesync_worker() || am_leader_apply_worker());
4783
4784	/* Setup signal handling */
4785	pqsignal(SIGHUP, SignalHandlerForConfigReload);
4786	pqsignal(SIGTERM, die);
4787	BackgroundWorkerUnblockSignals();
4788
4789	/*
4790	 * We don't currently need any ResourceOwner in a walreceiver process, but
4791	 * if we did, we could call CreateAuxProcessResourceOwner here.
4792	 */
4793
4794	/* Initialise stats to a sanish value */
4795	MyLogicalRepWorker->last_send_time = MyLogicalRepWorker->last_recv_time =
4796		MyLogicalRepWorker->reply_time = GetCurrentTimestamp();
4797
4798	/* Load the libpq-specific functions */
4799	load_file("libpqwalreceiver", false);
4800
4801	InitializeLogRepWorker();
4802
4803	/* Connect to the origin and start the replication. */
4804	elog(DEBUG1, "connecting to publisher using connection string \"%s\"",
4805		 MySubscription->conninfo);
4806
4807	/*
4808	 * Setup callback for syscache so that we know when something changes in
4809	 * the subscription relation state.
4810	 */
4811	CacheRegisterSyscacheCallback(SUBSCRIPTIONRELMAP,
4812								  invalidate_syncing_table_states,
4813								  (Datum) 0);
4814}
4815
4816/* Logical Replication Apply worker entry point */
4817void
4818ApplyWorkerMain(Datum main_arg)
4819{
4820	int			worker_slot = DatumGetInt32(main_arg);
4821
4822	InitializingApplyWorker = true;
4823
4824	SetupApplyOrSyncWorker(worker_slot);
4825
4826	InitializingApplyWorker = false;
4827
4828	run_apply_worker();
4829
4830	proc_exit(0);
4831}
4832
4833/*
4834 * After error recovery, disable the subscription in a new transaction
4835 * and exit cleanly.
4836 */
4837void
4838DisableSubscriptionAndExit(void)
4839{
4840	/*
4841	 * Emit the error message, and recover from the error state to an idle
4842	 * state
4843	 */
4844	HOLD_INTERRUPTS();
4845
4846	EmitErrorReport();
4847	AbortOutOfAnyTransaction();
4848	FlushErrorState();
4849
4850	RESUME_INTERRUPTS();
4851
4852	/* Report the worker failed during either table synchronization or apply */
4853	pgstat_report_subscription_error(MyLogicalRepWorker->subid,
4854									 !am_tablesync_worker());
4855
4856	/* Disable the subscription */
4857	StartTransactionCommand();
4858
4859	/*
4860	 * Updating pg_subscription might involve TOAST table access, so ensure we
4861	 * have a valid snapshot.
4862	 */
4863	PushActiveSnapshot(GetTransactionSnapshot());
4864
4865	DisableSubscription(MySubscription->oid);
4866	PopActiveSnapshot();
4867	CommitTransactionCommand();
4868
4869	/* Ensure we remove no-longer-useful entry for worker's start time */
4870	if (am_leader_apply_worker())
4871		ApplyLauncherForgetWorkerStartTime(MyLogicalRepWorker->subid);
4872
4873	/* Notify the subscription has been disabled and exit */
4874	ereport(LOG,
4875			errmsg("subscription \"%s\" has been disabled because of an error",
4876				   MySubscription->name));
4877
4878	proc_exit(0);
4879}
4880
4881/*
4882 * Is current process a logical replication worker?
4883 */
4884bool
4885IsLogicalWorker(void)
4886{
4887	return MyLogicalRepWorker != NULL;
4888}
4889
4890/*
4891 * Is current process a logical replication parallel apply worker?
4892 */
4893bool
4894IsLogicalParallelApplyWorker(void)
4895{
4896	return IsLogicalWorker() && am_parallel_apply_worker();
4897}
4898
4899/*
4900 * Start skipping changes of the transaction if the given LSN matches the
4901 * LSN specified by subscription's skiplsn.
4902 */
4903static void
4904maybe_start_skipping_changes(XLogRecPtr finish_lsn)
4905{
4906	Assert(!is_skipping_changes());
4907	Assert(!in_remote_transaction);
4908	Assert(!in_streamed_transaction);
4909
4910	/*
4911	 * Quick return if it's not requested to skip this transaction. This
4912	 * function is called for every remote transaction and we assume that
4913	 * skipping the transaction is not used often.
4914	 */
4915	if (likely(XLogRecPtrIsInvalid(MySubscription->skiplsn) ||
4916			   MySubscription->skiplsn != finish_lsn))
4917		return;
4918
4919	/* Start skipping all changes of this transaction */
4920	skip_xact_finish_lsn = finish_lsn;
4921
4922	ereport(LOG,
4923			errmsg("logical replication starts skipping transaction at LSN %X/%X",
4924				   LSN_FORMAT_ARGS(skip_xact_finish_lsn)));
4925}
4926
4927/*
4928 * Stop skipping changes by resetting skip_xact_finish_lsn if enabled.
4929 */
4930static void
4931stop_skipping_changes(void)
4932{
4933	if (!is_skipping_changes())
4934		return;
4935
4936	ereport(LOG,
4937			(errmsg("logical replication completed skipping transaction at LSN %X/%X",
4938					LSN_FORMAT_ARGS(skip_xact_finish_lsn))));
4939
4940	/* Stop skipping changes */
