4941	skip_xact_finish_lsn = InvalidXLogRecPtr;
4942}
4943
4944/*
4945 * Clear subskiplsn of pg_subscription catalog.
4946 *
4947 * finish_lsn is the transaction's finish LSN that is used to check if the
4948 * subskiplsn matches it. If not matched, we raise a warning when clearing the
4949 * subskiplsn in order to inform users for cases e.g., where the user mistakenly
4950 * specified the wrong subskiplsn.
4951 */
4952static void
4953clear_subscription_skip_lsn(XLogRecPtr finish_lsn)
4954{
4955	Relation	rel;
4956	Form_pg_subscription subform;
4957	HeapTuple	tup;
4958	XLogRecPtr	myskiplsn = MySubscription->skiplsn;
4959	bool		started_tx = false;
4960
4961	if (likely(XLogRecPtrIsInvalid(myskiplsn)) || am_parallel_apply_worker())
4962		return;
4963
4964	if (!IsTransactionState())
4965	{
4966		StartTransactionCommand();
4967		started_tx = true;
4968	}
4969
4970	/*
4971	 * Updating pg_subscription might involve TOAST table access, so ensure we
4972	 * have a valid snapshot.
4973	 */
4974	PushActiveSnapshot(GetTransactionSnapshot());
4975
4976	/*
4977	 * Protect subskiplsn of pg_subscription from being concurrently updated
4978	 * while clearing it.
4979	 */
4980	LockSharedObject(SubscriptionRelationId, MySubscription->oid, 0,
4981					 AccessShareLock);
4982
4983	rel = table_open(SubscriptionRelationId, RowExclusiveLock);
4984
4985	/* Fetch the existing tuple. */
4986	tup = SearchSysCacheCopy1(SUBSCRIPTIONOID,
4987							  ObjectIdGetDatum(MySubscription->oid));
4988
4989	if (!HeapTupleIsValid(tup))
4990		elog(ERROR, "subscription \"%s\" does not exist", MySubscription->name);
4991
4992	subform = (Form_pg_subscription) GETSTRUCT(tup);
4993
4994	/*
4995	 * Clear the subskiplsn. If the user has already changed subskiplsn before
4996	 * clearing it we don't update the catalog and the replication origin
4997	 * state won't get advanced. So in the worst case, if the server crashes
4998	 * before sending an acknowledgment of the flush position the transaction
4999	 * will be sent again and the user needs to set subskiplsn again. We can
5000	 * reduce the possibility by logging a replication origin WAL record to
5001	 * advance the origin LSN instead but there is no way to advance the
5002	 * origin timestamp and it doesn't seem to be worth doing anything about
5003	 * it since it's a very rare case.
5004	 */
5005	if (subform->subskiplsn == myskiplsn)
5006	{
5007		bool		nulls[Natts_pg_subscription];
5008		bool		replaces[Natts_pg_subscription];
5009		Datum		values[Natts_pg_subscription];
5010
5011		memset(values, 0, sizeof(values));
5012		memset(nulls, false, sizeof(nulls));
5013		memset(replaces, false, sizeof(replaces));
5014
5015		/* reset subskiplsn */
5016		values[Anum_pg_subscription_subskiplsn - 1] = LSNGetDatum(InvalidXLogRecPtr);
5017		replaces[Anum_pg_subscription_subskiplsn - 1] = true;
5018
5019		tup = heap_modify_tuple(tup, RelationGetDescr(rel), values, nulls,
5020								replaces);
5021		CatalogTupleUpdate(rel, &tup->t_self, tup);
5022
5023		if (myskiplsn != finish_lsn)
5024			ereport(WARNING,
5025					errmsg("skip-LSN of subscription \"%s\" cleared", MySubscription->name),
5026					errdetail("Remote transaction's finish WAL location (LSN) %X/%X did not match skip-LSN %X/%X.",
5027							  LSN_FORMAT_ARGS(finish_lsn),
5028							  LSN_FORMAT_ARGS(myskiplsn)));
5029	}
5030
5031	heap_freetuple(tup);
5032	table_close(rel, NoLock);
5033
5034	PopActiveSnapshot();
5035
5036	if (started_tx)
5037		CommitTransactionCommand();
5038}
5039
5040/* Error callback to give more context info about the change being applied */
5041void
5042apply_error_callback(void *arg)
5043{
5044	ApplyErrorCallbackArg *errarg = &apply_error_callback_arg;
5045
5046	if (apply_error_callback_arg.command == 0)
5047		return;
5048
5049	Assert(errarg->origin_name);
5050
5051	if (errarg->rel == NULL)
5052	{
5053		if (!TransactionIdIsValid(errarg->remote_xid))
5054			errcontext("processing remote data for replication origin \"%s\" during message type \"%s\"",
5055					   errarg->origin_name,
5056					   logicalrep_message_type(errarg->command));
5057		else if (XLogRecPtrIsInvalid(errarg->finish_lsn))
5058			errcontext("processing remote data for replication origin \"%s\" during message type \"%s\" in transaction %u",
5059					   errarg->origin_name,
5060					   logicalrep_message_type(errarg->command),
5061					   errarg->remote_xid);
5062		else
5063			errcontext("processing remote data for replication origin \"%s\" during message type \"%s\" in transaction %u, finished at %X/%X",
5064					   errarg->origin_name,
5065					   logicalrep_message_type(errarg->command),
5066					   errarg->remote_xid,
5067					   LSN_FORMAT_ARGS(errarg->finish_lsn));
5068	}
5069	else
5070	{
5071		if (errarg->remote_attnum < 0)
5072		{
5073			if (XLogRecPtrIsInvalid(errarg->finish_lsn))
5074				errcontext("processing remote data for replication origin \"%s\" during message type \"%s\" for replication target relation \"%s.%s\" in transaction %u",
5075						   errarg->origin_name,
5076						   logicalrep_message_type(errarg->command),
5077						   errarg->rel->remoterel.nspname,
5078						   errarg->rel->remoterel.relname,
5079						   errarg->remote_xid);
5080			else
5081				errcontext("processing remote data for replication origin \"%s\" during message type \"%s\" for replication target relation \"%s.%s\" in transaction %u, finished at %X/%X",
5082						   errarg->origin_name,
5083						   logicalrep_message_type(errarg->command),
5084						   errarg->rel->remoterel.nspname,
5085						   errarg->rel->remoterel.relname,
5086						   errarg->remote_xid,
5087						   LSN_FORMAT_ARGS(errarg->finish_lsn));
5088		}
5089		else
5090		{
5091			if (XLogRecPtrIsInvalid(errarg->finish_lsn))
5092				errcontext("processing remote data for replication origin \"%s\" during message type \"%s\" for replication target relation \"%s.%s\" column \"%s\" in transaction %u",
5093						   errarg->origin_name,
5094						   logicalrep_message_type(errarg->command),
5095						   errarg->rel->remoterel.nspname,
5096						   errarg->rel->remoterel.relname,
5097						   errarg->rel->remoterel.attnames[errarg->remote_attnum],
5098						   errarg->remote_xid);
5099			else
5100				errcontext("processing remote data for replication origin \"%s\" during message type \"%s\" for replication target relation \"%s.%s\" column \"%s\" in transaction %u, finished at %X/%X",
5101						   errarg->origin_name,
5102						   logicalrep_message_type(errarg->command),
5103						   errarg->rel->remoterel.nspname,
5104						   errarg->rel->remoterel.relname,
5105						   errarg->rel->remoterel.attnames[errarg->remote_attnum],
5106						   errarg->remote_xid,
5107						   LSN_FORMAT_ARGS(errarg->finish_lsn));
5108		}
5109	}
5110}
5111
5112/* Set transaction information of apply error callback */
5113static inline void
5114set_apply_error_context_xact(TransactionId xid, XLogRecPtr lsn)
5115{
5116	apply_error_callback_arg.remote_xid = xid;
5117	apply_error_callback_arg.finish_lsn = lsn;
5118}
5119
5120/* Reset all information of apply error callback */
5121static inline void
5122reset_apply_error_context_info(void)
5123{
5124	apply_error_callback_arg.command = 0;
5125	apply_error_callback_arg.rel = NULL;
5126	apply_error_callback_arg.remote_attnum = -1;
5127	set_apply_error_context_xact(InvalidTransactionId, InvalidXLogRecPtr);
5128}
5129
5130/*
5131 * Request wakeup of the workers for the given subscription OID
5132 * at commit of the current transaction.
5133 *
5134 * This is used to ensure that the workers process assorted changes
5135 * as soon as possible.
5136 */
5137void
5138LogicalRepWorkersWakeupAtCommit(Oid subid)
5139{
5140	MemoryContext oldcxt;
5141
5142	oldcxt = MemoryContextSwitchTo(TopTransactionContext);
5143	on_commit_wakeup_workers_subids =
5144		list_append_unique_oid(on_commit_wakeup_workers_subids, subid);
5145	MemoryContextSwitchTo(oldcxt);
5146}
5147
5148/*
5149 * Wake up the workers of any subscriptions that were changed in this xact.
5150 */
5151void
5152AtEOXact_LogicalRepWorkers(bool isCommit)
5153{
5154	if (isCommit && on_commit_wakeup_workers_subids != NIL)
5155	{
5156		ListCell   *lc;
5157
5158		LWLockAcquire(LogicalRepWorkerLock, LW_SHARED);
5159		foreach(lc, on_commit_wakeup_workers_subids)
5160		{
5161			Oid			subid = lfirst_oid(lc);
5162			List	   *workers;
5163			ListCell   *lc2;
5164
5165			workers = logicalrep_workers_find(subid, true, false);
5166			foreach(lc2, workers)
5167			{
5168				LogicalRepWorker *worker = (LogicalRepWorker *) lfirst(lc2);
5169
5170				logicalrep_worker_wakeup_ptr(worker);
5171			}
5172		}
5173		LWLockRelease(LogicalRepWorkerLock);
5174	}
5175
5176	/* The List storage will be reclaimed automatically in xact cleanup. */
5177	on_commit_wakeup_workers_subids = NIL;
5178}
5179
5180/*
5181 * Allocate the origin name in long-lived context for error context message.
5182 */
5183void
5184set_apply_error_context_origin(char *originname)
5185{
5186	apply_error_callback_arg.origin_name = MemoryContextStrdup(ApplyContext,
5187															   originname);
5188}
5189
5190/*
5191 * Return the action to be taken for the given transaction. See
5192 * TransApplyAction for information on each of the actions.
5193 *
5194 * *winfo is assigned to the destination parallel worker info when the leader
5195 * apply worker has to pass all the transaction's changes to the parallel
5196 * apply worker.
5197 */
5198static TransApplyAction
5199get_transaction_apply_action(TransactionId xid, ParallelApplyWorkerInfo **winfo)
5200{
5201	*winfo = NULL;
5202
5203	if (am_parallel_apply_worker())
5204	{
5205		return TRANS_PARALLEL_APPLY;
5206	}
5207
5208	/*
5209	 * If we are processing this transaction using a parallel apply worker
5210	 * then either we send the changes to the parallel worker or if the worker
5211	 * is busy then serialize the changes to the file which will later be
5212	 * processed by the parallel worker.
5213	 */
5214	*winfo = pa_find_worker(xid);
5215
5216	if (*winfo && (*winfo)->serialize_changes)
5217	{
5218		return TRANS_LEADER_PARTIAL_SERIALIZE;
5219	}
5220	else if (*winfo)
5221	{
5222		return TRANS_LEADER_SEND_TO_PARALLEL;
5223	}
5224
5225	/*
5226	 * If there is no parallel worker involved to process this transaction
5227	 * then we either directly apply the change or serialize it to a file
5228	 * which will later be applied when the transaction finish message is
5229	 * processed.
5230	 */
5231	else if (in_streamed_transaction)
5232	{
5233		return TRANS_LEADER_SERIALIZE;
5234	}
5235	else
5236	{
5237		return TRANS_LEADER_APPLY;
5238	}
5239}
